title: Hattie and the Mystery of the Muddy Mountain
summary: While foraging for hat-making materials in the forest, Hattie the Mouse hears a cry for help and discovers her friend Mischief Mouse is trapped inside a mountain. After navigating through secret entrances and dark, muddy tunnels, Hattie finds Mischief stuck in a deep patch of mud. Using water from a nearby underground stream to wash the mud away, Hattie successfully rescues her friend. The pair collects some underground mushrooms and escapes back into the sunlight, eventually heading home for a much-needed rest.

highlightQuote.text: Keep telling the story and then you'll find out.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Now, I'm not sure what kind of a story we should tell. What do you think?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: We should tell one about Hattie the Mouse.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay. Do you have any requests for what kind of adventure Hattie goes on?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No. We just keep telling the story and then...

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay. Well, once upon a time, Hattie the Mouse was walking through the forest looking for ingredients for hats, because she needed lots of material to make hats out of. But suddenly, while she was digging through a patch of beautiful little ferns to try to find just the right fern, because a fern could be nice on a hat, she heard a funny squeaky snuffling sound. What do you think that sound was?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It was Mischief Mouse.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Mischief Mouse was going, 'Help me, help, I'm trapped!'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Hattie the Mouse said, 'Oh no, it sounds like Mischief Mouse is in trouble. I better help her.' And what did Mischief Mouse say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Help me!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Hattie said, 'What's the matter, Mischief? I don't even see you. Where are you?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Mischief!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Mischief, where are you? And what did Mischief Mouse say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'm right in here!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: You're right in there? Where?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Inside.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Inside...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Inside this mountain.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: You're inside the mountain?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And there's a little door.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And there's a little door. Oh.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, so I'm trapped in the mountain.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: You're trapped inside the mountain, said Hattie. Well, let me help you out. And so Hattie walked back and forth around the mountain looking for the door. And finally...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, the door is right in the back of the mountain.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, Hattie said, thanks for letting me know it's right in the back. And so she walked around to the back of the mountain and she saw the door. It was a little round door and it was made of wood and it had a little handle. But it also had a sign on it. What did the sign say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Keep telling the story and then you'll find out.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: The sign on the door said keep telling the story and then you'll find out? Hattie said, 'Huh, that's funny. It's almost like I'm inside of a story.' Well, I better find out what happens. So Hattie pulled on the handle, but it wouldn't open. And she pulled harder. 'Oh no,' she said, 'I can't open the door and Mischief is trapped inside. What can I do?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But there's a big hole on the front, and there's a little hole on the front of the mountain.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Hattie said, 'Aha! Thanks for telling me, Mischief Mouse. I don't have to get in through the door which seems to be locked anyway. I can go around the front of the mountain and go into the hole.' And so Hattie went around to the front and climbed in the hole. And as she climbed into the hole, she felt something squishy under her feet. What was it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Keep telling the story and then you'll find out.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It was just some mud. And so she went squish squish squish through the mud as she went through the tunnel. And then she turned a corner and she saw a strange green glowing light. What was it that was glowing inside the tunnel?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It was Mischief.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Mischief Mouse was glowing? Why is Mischief Mouse glowing?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Because she had a flashlight.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh! And Hattie says, 'Wow, I thought I was about to see some interesting glowing underground thing, like maybe some glow worms or glowing mushrooms or strange glowing underground fireflies. But no, it was just you with your flashlight, Mischief Mouse.' And what did Mischief Mouse say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Help, I'm stuck in the mud!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, you're stuck inside. 'But I'm here to rescue you,' said Hattie. And Hattie looked down at Mischief Mouse's feet and they were stuck. How were they stuck? Were they tied up or were they chained up or were they in some mud? How did Mischief Mouse get stuck?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She got stuck in mud, mud, mud.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, so Hattie the Mouse said, 'Oh, it looks like you're stuck in some mud. But I have a little bucket here, and I hear a trickle of water coming from nearby. I think it's an underground stream.' She ran off and filled up her bucket and then came back and splashed the mud with her water to wash off Mischief's feet. And then she was free! Her feet popped out of the mud. And Hattie said, 'What were you doing in here that you ended up getting stuck, Mischief Mouse? What were you looking for?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I was looking for something to eat.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh! And Hattie says, 'Well, I don't see much to eat in here.' I guess there's some interesting tiny little mushrooms around. Let's pick a few. And so they picked a few underground mushrooms, and then Mischief said, 'Let's get out of here.' And Hattie said, 'Yeah! Should we try to go out the door?' And Mischief said, 'Okay, let's try it.' And so they went down the dark tunnel, and they found the other side of the door and they pushed it right open because they could unlock it from the inside. And then the bright glow of sunlight hit their eyes, and they popped right out. Mischief and Hattie came out through the door and they were out of the tunnel and free once again. And what did they do next?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I think this is the end of the story.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I think you're right, it is the end of the story. They were very tired from their adventure, and so they both went to their homes and got in their cozy beds and fell asleep.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, Daddy, is it okay if you turn on the lamp?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah, I'll turn on the lamp now.

characters.name: Hattie the Mouse
characters.description: A nimble, friendly mouse of Possible Street and loyal friend to Izzy and Seeker, this brave artisan is the wife of Dimitar and mistress of the Workshop of Hats—a weathered hollow tree sanctuary on Cornell Street scented with cedar, ink, and peanut butter toast. Clad in her signature red cloak and pointy hat, she is a resourceful hatmaker whose spirit was forged against massive diggers and the rain-slicked Floofer Cloud. Within her paint-stained home, she carefully curates her husband’s library alongside her own hand-painted volumes while confronting a baffling supply mystery; the discovery of curious clover stickers signals a gathering shadow of intrigue she is determined to unravel.

characters.name: Mischief Mouse
characters.description: Reigning from a purple-striped Jovian castle, this affable mouse King—a playful-named companion to Pinecone Mouse—is a seasoned adventurer, guardian of the Underground Library, and Hattie’s steadfast ally. Between savoring artisanal cheeses and awaiting a rugged cardboard replacement for his fragile Lego hat, he spends leisure hours meticulously constructing and gleefully smashing soaring towers. A pancake-loving monarch who once tended shop for mountain-dwelling traffic cones, his cosmic quests recently shifted from the stars to the speedway; having borrowed a tow truck from Mistress Mouse, he proved a formidable competitor who nearly clinched a stunning victory. Whether piloting starships or joining the group’s latest trek to Nana’s house, he remains a vigilant protector and devoted friend across the galaxy.

places.name: The Forest
places.description: Cloaking the surface of the green planet in a vast mantle of dense greenery, this whispering woodland serves as the lush setting of the 'Forest Friends' chronicle. It is a realm where creatures play eternal hide-and-seek between Hattie’s cozy clinic and the ancestral cradle of Finn’s companions. Along magical trails where Hattie and the Pakruder Worm harvest hat-making ingredients, winding paths veil secret gopher tunnels and hidden mole cottages, forming the essential route toward the wizard's castle. While its sun-drenched threshold remains a sanctuary for the Rainbow Giraffe and Mama Bird, the deeper, darker thickets harbor a somber mystery; it was within these sprawling, uncharted depths—the same eerie expanse where the sick creature was first spotted and Mossy was discovered—that Mole recently vanished into the shadows.

places.name: The Mountain
places.description: A large structure containing hidden tunnels, secret holes, and a wooden door at the back.

places.name: The Muddy Tunnel
places.description: A dark underground passage where the ground is squishy and difficult to navigate.

places.name: Underground Stream
places.description: A hidden source of water found deep within the mountain.
